title: crepis foetida subsp. glandulosa
intro: Crepis foetida subsp. glandulosa, also known as the glandular hawk's beard or stinking hawk's beard, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Crepis foetida subsp. glandulosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
